edges.target: Constitutive STING signaling and type I interferonopathy

edges.source: Constitutive STING signaling and type I interferonopathy
Constitutive STING signaling and type I interferonopathy
edges.target: Progressive interstitial lung disease and fibrosis

nodes.description: SAVI is characterized by constitutive STING-driven IFNB1 transcription and persistent interferon activation in patient immune cells.
nodes.id: Constitutive STING signaling and type I interferonopathy

evidence.reference_title: Activated STING in a vascular and pulmonary syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Elevated transcription of IFNB1 and other gene targets of STING in peripheral-blood mononuclear cells from the patients indicated constitutive activation of the pathway that cannot be further up-regulated with stimulation.

evidence.reference_title: Overview of STING-Associated Vasculopathy with Onset in Infancy (SAVI) Among 21 Patients.
evidence.snippet: Increased expression of interferon-stimulated genes and interferon α protein was constant.

nodes.description: STING activation in endothelial cells promotes endothelial activation and apoptosis, providing a mechanistic basis for the characteristic cutaneous vasculopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Activated STING in a vascular and pulmonary syndrome.
evidence.snippet: STING is expressed in endothelial cells, and exposure of these cells to cGAMP resulted in endothelial activation and apoptosis.

nodes.description: Pulmonary disease begins early, can show fibrotic change on imaging, and is the leading cause of morbidity and mortality in SAVI.
nodes.id: Progressive interstitial lung disease and fibrosis

evidence.reference_title: Overview of STING-Associated Vasculopathy with Onset in Infancy (SAVI) Among 21 Patients.
evidence.snippet: Severity of ILD was highly variable with insidious progression up to end-stage respiratory failure reached at teenage in 6 patients. Lung imaging revealed early fibrotic lesions.

evidence.reference_title: Lung Inflammation in STING-Associated Vasculopathy with Onset in Infancy (SAVI).
evidence.snippet: Lung disease is the leading cause of morbidity and mortality in these two disorders which do not respond to conventional immunosuppressive therapies and only partially to JAK1/2 inhibitors.

nodes.description: Activating STING1/TMEM173 variants constitutively activate the STING pathway, establishing SAVI as a type I interferonopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Activated STING in a vascular and pulmonary syndrome.
evidence.snippet: STING-associated vasculopathy with onset in infancy (SAVI) is an autoinflammatory disease caused by gain-of-function mutations in TMEM173.

evidence.reference_title: Activated STING in a vascular and pulmonary syndrome.
evidence.snippet: STING-associated vasculopathy with onset in infancy (SAVI) is an autoinflammatory disease caused by gain-of-function mutations in TMEM173.

evidence.reference_title: Phenotypic spectrum in recessive STING-associated vasculopathy with onset in infancy: Four novel cases and analysis of previously reported cases.
evidence.snippet: Previously, only heterozygous and mostly de novo STING1 variants have been reported to cause SAVI. Interestingly, one variant that only leads to SAVI when homozygous, namely c.841C>T p.(Arg281Trp), has recently been described.
